Name: -1_to_and_for_the
Representative_Docs: ["My family lives in Las Cruces and I live in Socorro five days out of the week to work here. I leave for work at 5 am on Monday and go back home 5 pm on Friday. I have a toddler and a girlfriend going to university full-time. I can't marry because they'll lose the goverment assistance they need to get by. They can't move here because there is no support system in Soccorro like there is for them in Cruces. \nI am spending money on two rents. I'm in a very unique situation, but it's difficult and it doesn't seem like the university understands. This paycheck isn't enough and the department I work in is going to keep having problems filling this role because people are going to take this job and move on when another place offers more. That's not efficient because the job I have requires a lot of training to become proficient. ", "Northern needs to pay their staff more. We do so much in our office who is short staffed and we are not getting enough pay. My office as well as others in Student Services have been stressed and tired and some just don't think its is worth it to stay here, that is why we lose so many good collogues. We also should be able to receive a certain amount of mental health days to use to recuperate and destress. If we were paid more, we wouldn't have to worry about not paying our rent/mortgage or bills on time, which would cut down on stress therefore increasing better productivity in all offices ", 'I have a good support system and am lucky enough to receive Medicaid, but if I did not have those I cannot imagine where I would be. Many people attending universities have a lot less. UNM has some programs to help their students, but they are still very expensive which is by far the biggest obstacle for students. I attended college there in my mid 20s so I had no scholarship even though I was a great student with great grades. I now have student loans I will be paying off for the next few decades. Making college classes less expensive, having cheaper parking passes, and better scholarships would go a long way to making housing and food more secure. SO much money went into my education and even more with interest will, too. Books, supplies, transportation, less working hours, and time away from home all cost money, too.']

Name: 3_pantry_food_campus_the
Representative_Docs:
["Food desert studies
Food trucks
Agriculture projects
By in security It's short lived it was because of a natural disaster and unaccounted for event. But the things I hear from my students give me nightmares AB one student on the Hopi reservation in 2021 who called me to tell me what the variety of fresh vegetables they had they went into a gas station which is the closest place to buy food by hours to where this person lives And they took a picture of what they showed me was really disgusting looking bell peppers and onions some garlic and some potatoes. That's all they had on the shelf. And I mean it wasn't even as much as what I would put in my refrigerator for my family of 4 for 2 or 3 days and that was all they had access to after traveling for hours. I'll never get over the things I heard during a pandemic from my students I'll never forget them telling me on Easten that they didn't have baby formula or diapers had to drive hours and hours it's kind of sneakout and go camp near grocery stores so that they could hit up all the grocery stores they could to get as much of the stuff that they could to bring it back to the reservation. I'll never forget when my student who had 4 kids of their own and the their sister commit suicide and so she's not getting all of her kids so instantly she has 7 kids and it's the pandemic and she couldn't get formula I mean 3 of the 7 kids were under 2. I only want to be concerned with this teaching people I don't have to be thinking about if they have diapers and far below food for their kids. That's why during 2020 I grew a lot of vegetables between the rows of my hip and I don't need those vegetables to local shelters homeless shelters and I trucked some out to the nation. The conditions of boys benefit or miss from a students there's food deserts there's inequity in food lake at a level that is unpalatable For any caring human being. That's why I think we should have food trucks and it should be fresh fruits and fresh vegetables and we should have agricultural projects where we teach people to grow fruits of vegetables. And to help farmers distribute their product and to have more diversity in what they have and give them places for their produce to be sold directly. ", 'Have a food pantry on campus', 'Have a campus food pantry']

Name: 23_students_housing_financial_provide
Representative_Docs:
['Provide emergency housing and financial assistance; organize better access to free and low-cost food options; subsidize housing and food for students, staff, and faculty in-need; train and teach healthy nutrition along with financial, legal, and mental wellness.  Also, provide research and advocacy in all of these areas. ', 'Colleges and universities can help address food and housing insecurity in several ways. These can include providing emergency food and housing assistance, increasing access to affordable housing, providing free food pantries, and offering education and programs to help students better manage their finances. Additionally, schools can partner with outside organizations to provide students with additional resources, such as legal aid and mental health support.', 'I teach one class at a charter high school in the south valley. I take food there regularly because they don't "like" the food the school serves and always complain they are hungry, so they gladly take my packaged food which is primarily junk food. But I figure a treat is a treat, and if they know someone cares, that could mean a lot to them. The systems aren't working for everyone. I'm so lucky to be in such a place of privilege that I didn't earn. I lead a very privileged life compared to the students at the charter school. For example:
There was a shooting a few weeks ago. Two students were killed. Another student misses her dad who left her a decade ago to go to Mexico once he was "wanted" and has come back to the area but hasn't responded that he wants to see her. Another student lost a 2-year-old cousin about 2 weeks ago. Most all live in single parent homes. Apartments. South Valley houses with extended families sometimes. These kids have a very hard life, right here in Albuquerque where we ought to be able to do better. 
I share this because I can see the disparity and it breaks my heart. 
Thanks for initiating this survey to see what needs are out there.']

Name: 41_insecurity_affect_work_affects
Representative_Docs: ["If food or housing is not safe, it can affect an employee's work in many ways. For example, people who live in unsafe housing may struggle to get enough rest or suffer from health conditions due to inadequate living standards. Low-quality food can also lead to malnutrition or illness, which can disrupt one's work performance. Additionally, if a family is financially strained due to high housing costs or the need to purchase safe housing, the employee may struggle to focus at work or use too much of their salary just to meet basic needs.", 'Food and housing insecurity does not affect my work', 'Food or housing insecurity does not affect my work.k ']

Unfortunately, I have had to make the hard decision to leave UNM to pursue other employment opportunities based on higher wages, less retirement deductions and no parking fees. UNM entity is a great establishment I am just so sad I have to leave based on my current needs financially. I am praying I can come back once all this financial hardship passes. I love the campus!  ', 'Contribute to existing efforts! The Dean of Students Office and LoboRESPECT Advocacy Center are already doing so much to help with food and housing insecurity! Name: 56_insecurity_should_housing_community
Representative_Docs: ["Thank you for looking into this issue. It affects all members of out community directly and indirectly, even those like me who aren't currently experiencing food insecurity and housing uncertainty.", "Completing this survey revealed just how lucky I currently am to not be experiencing food or housing insecurity, I want to help those that are affected, but I'm not sure how to have an impact and help those in my community. It is horrible and shocking that people among us are experiencing these insecurities.", 'Food and Housing insecurity is a wide spread issue and has a certain class stigma attached to it. The best way to overcome stigmas is to talk about the issue, not about who or why. I believe our places of public gatherings should offer space for the community to provide for themselves. These places that were built for the community should have community gardens and instead of decorative vegetation, food producing trees and plants should be in abundance. In the harvest season, the community should be able to use a shared space to practice food preservation. In the off season (winter) there should be green houses or high tunnels where winter crops could be grown. Extra food should be given to the food pantries. It is crazy to me that 50% of the food is thrown in the trash while others do not know where their next bite will come from.']

Representative_Docs: ['Again, colleges and universities have been corporatized to the point of becoming diploma mills that charge too much tuition/fees and suffer from cognitive dissonance in terms of students needs by superficially providing support but not LOVE. ', 'Make college more affordable, including tuition, housing, books, board, fees. The Khan Academy, for example, provides subject-matter lessons that are totally free, are available on demand, and are mostly far better at conveying useful information than in-class courses available at practically any of our public universities.']

Name: 66_transportation_were_bus_buses
Representative_Docs: ["I appreciate that this survey exists. I think the public transportation piece is really important. We were excited about the Blue Bus, but haven't heard any updates. Is that still happening? It would be great if we could have a UNM-Taos shuttle that goes into town. It would be great advertising for UNM-Taos and could maybe even increase enrollment. ", "I do not have such problems. However please consider whether organized commutes to & from El Paso or Hatch couldn't help students who live there but study at NMSU-Las Cruces. I regularly have students miss class because of mechanical issues on the road. There are buses between El Paso & Las Cruces, but it's worth asking whether options couldn't be improved either by working more closely with existing busing options or creating new ones.", 'I Having been a student in the bad old days some of the issues with food was that I did not qualify for any assistance,  My undergraduate was from Texas A&M and they had a great program from former students that supplied loans that did not have interest to pay tuition which could be paid off at the end of semester.  There were enough jobs around that I could work several and pay it off.  Graduated without debt.  Here there are not enough jobs even though the city is much bigger and federal loans is the only way, especially for graduate students, to make ends meet.  The grad students at Texas had health insurance which eased my burden.  Of course, the state had approved such a thing.  \n\nOne of my biggest peeves was from last years ice storm.  Students were stranded on campus and UNM did not do anything.  Calling school closed late and no preparations was very dangerous.  I was busing students to my daughters trailer since she lives on south campus.  CIty buses were stranded and I only found out later that the sun van has 4-wheel drive.  Still there were students that were stuck at the library and had no way home and the library was closing.  I would love to see a plan if students are trapped where they can shelter until the danger passes and they can get off campus.']

We have a real problem here in Farmington.  The fast food industry is rampant, but not much of a good choice with respect to the food.  I would like to see healthier choices that would keep our community from diabetes, heart disease, and cancer.', 'I spend between $200 and 300 each semester bringing fruit, healthy snacks and treats to my classes because I know some students are hungry and that can help them stay focused.']

Name: 71_commute_home_move_my
Representative_Docs: ["I lost my last job because they changed my work location, which I couldn't do with my car that couldn't handle the commute and was shared, getting two people to and from work. ", "Housing is more so focused on my commute to work every day, especially when the weather is a little unpredictable during the winter, preventing me to telework or stay home. I am still called to work and leave during these times. I wish I had a home nearby that I could easily get to, rather than commuting an hour. Food-wise, I don't have enough time to get groceries and often neglect getting food due to stress or exhaustion from my day.", 'Consistently worried about being priced out of my apartment, do not take home enough after ERB and taxes to buy a home that is livable (acutally has wiring, roof not caving in, etc).  My apt building is falling down and owner makes no changes, when does do updates, raises rent.  I have a fridge that consistently stops cooling, so i lose my food.  I have an oven that jumps to 500degree randomly so I ruin/burn food.  These worries makes it hard to plan, prep, and save to find somewhere new.  All open listings of apartments are double or more what I pay now, so I cannot afford to move somewhere better or safer.  Do not want to move neighborhoods even if it is unsafe because if my car breaks I can still walk to work. ']

I definitely give a lot of extensions for folks who are having a hard time. I donâ€™t want them to give up on school and the chance for the life that they aspire to itâ€™s always a little heartbreaking when they have to drop a course or donâ€™t do very well, and I know that itâ€™s due to external factors such as food and housing insecurity for them or their families.

This is just something that I didnâ€™t see you 10 years ago and definitely nothing like it. 25 years ago.

People who work full time can still be struggling and trying to find ways to "stay above water" while taking care of their own bills and responsibilities to others.']

Name: 73_pay_lower_minimum_benefits
Representative_Docs:
["Pay better salaries to professions with college degrees that earn quite a bit more for the same job in other areas of the country. As our cost of living is not less here but likely more since we have the military base close by.
Otherwise, I do think that our university is involved in programs that help students with these issue and being more rural alot do have the 'neighborly' attitude and help when they can", "As someone who works for the university an answer that comes to mind is to pay people more, particularly people who are in lower paying jobs. I'm doing well but not everyone employed here is making a wage that would create security. If I had children or was caring for a family member inflation would have me on edge right now and I would perhaps need to earn more to feel secure. ", 'Be more open to diversity when hiring and paying employees and giving that extension to students who may become employees. I have applied to internal jobs for which I am qualified (or very slightly under qualified). There is a clear bias (gender, personality, ability to manipulate) in who is hired. 
Diversity when paying, I have more qualifications, experience, and oversee more projects than anyone however the degree is what determines the pay (I cannot afford to pursue a new degree even if it benefits the college). I can get work dones (when my mind is clear) 2-4 times faster, yet get payed per hour at the same rate. My tasks are complete and I get payed for 1 hour when someone else gets paid for 3 just cause it took them longer. 25% of my pay goes to taxes and more if I had insurance through them. Take home pay is 12 dollars and 13 dollars for many full time workers. 
I do not want free housing (I like my house I just need to afford it). We do not free food ( I see too much free food get thrown away. We need reasonable pay for living and buying what we need. When we pay for it, we endear it. (I am told the benefits outweigh the pay) the benefits are restricted and mean nothing if I cannot live in peace. 
As far as students, go if this continues, what hope do they have but to fall into the same net. They might have free food items today but do they know how to cook with what is free? Do they have the appliance, the cooking place, the utilities, the secure home? ']

Name: 75_rent_will_was_lease
Representative_Docs: ['More at the level of a constant worry... Will rent go up and force us to move? Will we make enough to pay all the bills and buy food for us and pets?  Will I get enough classes to be able to pay off what we have to put on credit card? That kind of thing.', 'I was living with family for financial reasons, but was forced to move out when it became dangerous. During that time, it was very difficult to focus at work because  I was always worried about what was going to be waiting for me at "home." At this point, I\'m in a better living situation, but a worse financial one. The rental property I was able to find is very expensive for how much I\'m making, and I\'m currently the only employed person in the household.', "Right now I'm at stable spot but I worry about new rental leasing with rent increases/landlords or property management not fulfilling their legal roles. My partner and I want to mortgage a house but it seems impossible. When we eventually face these issues I'm sure it will negatively affect my work."]
